{{Quote|With the exception of the unfortunate Komm Karsh, our forces have been victorious everywhere.|Tsavong Lah|<ref name="dw" />}}
'''Komm Karsh''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Warrior caste|warrior]] holding the rank of [[Supreme Commander (Yuuzhan Vong rank)|Supreme Commander]] in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]]. Under [[Supreme Overlord]] [[Shimrra Jamaane]], the Yuuzhan Vong [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invaded]] [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], and Karsh was the leader of the Vong's only strategic buffer in case of a major enemy reprisal. However, he [[Death/Legends|perished]] along with the majority of his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] in 28 ABY, during his [[Second Battle of Obroa-skai|attempt]] to examine the contents of the libraries of the [[First Battle of Obroa-skai|captured]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Obroa-skai/Legends|Obroa-skai]]. Karsh's death and the loss of his fleet forced the [[Yuuzhan Vong empire]] to refrain from offensive actions.

==Biography==
===Death at Obroa-skai===
{{Quote|And may I remind the Warmaster that Komm Karsh's fleet was our sole strategic reserve?|Shimrra Jamaane, to Tsavong Lah|<ref name="dw" />}}
Komm Karsh was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Warrior caste|warrior]] who lived during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Yuuzhan Vong invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="dw" /> In [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]],<ref name="TERC" /> Karsh held the rank of [[Supreme Commander (Yuuzhan Vong rank)|Supreme Commander]], and he presided over strategic provisions for the [[Yuuzhan Vong fleet]].<ref name="dw" /> At some point, he made a visit to the library [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Obroa-skai/Legends|Obroa-skai]] in order to extract unspecified intelligence from the libraries there.<ref name="dw" /><ref name="fh2">''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]''</ref> The [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] planet had been [[First Battle of Obroa-skai|captured]] by the Yuuzhan Vong forces led by [[Priest caste|priest]] [[Harrar]] and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Tla]]<ref name="aoc1">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]''</ref> in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]],<ref name="TERC" /> but the libraries were spared wholesale destruction as they provided a massive repository of knowledge for the Yuuzhan Vong in their conquest of the galaxy.<ref name="aoc1" />

[[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]], however, had received word that [[Supreme Overlord]] [[Shimrra Jamaane]] was expected at Obroa-skai, and although it was Karsh who visited the world instead, a trap was set for any Yuuzhan Vong [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] which would leap into the [[Obroa-skai system/Legends|Obroa-skai system]]. Karsh and his fleet entered the [[Star system/Legends|system]] from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] near the planet, leading the mission from [[Komm Karsh's flagship|his flagship]]. The arrival of a [[ro'ik chuun m'arh]] [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]], the [[Trickster|''Ksstarr'']], tailed by a weighty [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Task force/Legends|task force]] under the [[Agamar/Legends|Agamarian]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], shocked the Supreme Commander and his forces into battle formation. The [[yammosk]], a Yuuzhan Vong creature created to coordinate battles, onboard Karsh's [[Command ship/Legends|command vessel]] ordered the frigate ''Ksstarr'' to remain hidden in the formation as [[Yorik-et|coralskippers]] moved out to engage the New Republic forces, supported by another yammosk on the planet surface. Karsh matched Farlander's movements, drawing his fleet up to approach the enemy [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] with a wide firing angle. Karsh hung back in his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], guarded by a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] screen as his fleet engaged the enemy.<ref name="dw" />

[[File:ObroaSkai.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Obroa-skai, the world above which Karsh was killed]]
The Supreme Commander had his first yammosk [[Gravitic amplitude modulator|jammed]] by the New Republic forces, as on the surface of Obroa-skai, the Republic [[Commando/Legends|commando]] [[unit]] [[Wraith Squadron]] had found the planet's war-coordinator. Karsh had brought a second yammosk into battle, however, and his forces made use of the creature's coordination skills, as well as their numerical advantage, inflicting considerable damage to Farlander's forces even as New Republic technology caused the Yuuzhan Vong ships to fire upon one another. It was not long before the tide of the battle changed once again; the New Republic jammed the fleet's war-coordinator, and Karsh's forces fell into disarray. To compound matters, the frigate which Karsh had allowed to take up position at the stern of his vessel, the ''Ksstarr'',<ref name="dw" /> had in fact been captured by a Republic-allied [[Myrkr strike team|Jedi task force]].<ref name="sbs">''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]''</ref> Under the command of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], the ''Ksstarr'' opened fire on Karsh's command ship, obliterating the weaponry at the stern and altering the flagship's [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] signal to suggest that it was an enemy of the Yuuzhan Vong. Soon, Karsh's ship was overwhelmed by the concentrated firepower of its own forces, as well as that of the New Republic fleet. Although the Supreme Commander's other forces rushed back to help the stricken vessel, a [[Hapan]] fleet, led by [[Queen Mother]] [[Tenel Ka Djo]] and allied with the New Republic, leaped in-system and decimated Karsh's supporting ships. With a cataclysmic explosion, Karsh's flagship blew apart, [[Death/Legends|killing]] the Supreme Commander. Only one of Karsh's vessels escaped the ambush.<ref name="dw" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|From this point, moving any warrior to strengthen one force will weaken another.|Shimrra Jamaane|<ref name="dw" />}}
Komm Karsh's death—and the near-total destruction of his fleet—proved a bitter blow to the newly-christened [[Yuuzhan Vong empire]].<ref name="dw" /> Reeling from the costly [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|capture]] of the New Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],<ref name="sbs" /> failures in the [[Pyria system]]<ref name="el1">''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]''</ref><ref name="el2">''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand]]''</ref> and the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]],<ref name="dj">''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Journey]]''</ref> as well as the sabotage of the [[World Brain]] on the re-shaped galactic capital,<ref name="t">''[[The New Jedi Order: Traitor]]''</ref> Shimrra could not afford to lose such a large portion of the fleet. [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] was ordered to refrain from continued offensives, allowing the New Republic to regain a fighting chance and [[Battle of Ebaq|defeat]] the Yuuzhan Vong at [[Ebaq 9]].<ref name="dw" /> Meanwhile, [[Hreven Karsh]], a sibling of the deceased Supreme Commander, was promoted to the rank of commander to replenish the ranks, and led a failed attempt to capture a [[New Jedi Order|Jedi]] force in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]].<ref name="fh2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Komm Karsh never appears in person in [[Walter Jon Williams]]' ''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'', and is killed near the beginning of the novel; his death, however, has far-reaching consequences.<ref name="dw" /> It transpires in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]'' that Karsh's "position" has been filled by his sibling, Hreven Karsh, although for some reason the latter ascends not to the rank of Supreme Commander, but merely to that of commander.<ref name="fh2" />
